title: Replace Batteries
summary: Safely remove old batteries and insert new ones in the correct orientation.
learnerPromise: You will be able to safely remove old batteries and insert new ones with the correct polarity in common household devices.
whyItMatters: Many everyday devices run on batteries. Knowing how to replace them correctly keeps devices working and prevents damage from reversed polarity or leaking old batteries.
realLifeUses: Reviving a TV remote that stopped responding
Replacing dead batteries in a flashlight before a trip
Changing batteries in a smoke alarm or clock
Fixing a wireless mouse or keyboard that is acting sluggish
materialsNeeded: New batteries (correct size: AA, AAA, etc.)
Small screwdriver (if battery cover is screwed shut)
result: A device powered by correctly installed new batteries.
youWillLearn: How to open a battery compartment safely
How to identify the positive and negative ends of a battery
How to match battery polarity to the compartment markings
How to dispose of old batteries properly
miniChallenge: Find one device in your home with dead batteries and replace them today.
steps: Turn off the device and locate the battery compartment cover.
Open the cover by sliding it, pressing a tab, or unscrewing it gently.
Remove the old batteries, noting which direction they face.
Insert the new batteries matching the plus (+) and minus (-) symbols in the compartment.
Close the cover securely and test the device to confirm it works.
completionCriteria: New batteries are inserted with correct polarity as marked in the compartment
Device powers on and functions normally after closing the cover
commonProblems: Device still does not work — check that the polarity is correct and batteries are fresh
Battery cover is stuck — look for a hidden latch or tab, do not force it
Old battery is leaking — wear gloves if available, clean the compartment with a dry cloth, and wash hands after
tips: Always match the battery size on the device label exactly
Remove batteries from devices you will not use for several months
Recycle old batteries at a designated drop-off rather than throwing them in regular trash
